title: Head of Production - EV Division, EMEA
department: BMW Group Production - EV Division EMEA
role_summary:
BMW Group is seeking an experienced senior production leader to assume responsibility for the Head of Production - EV Division, EMEA role. This position carries end-to-end leadership accountability for production performance across the regional EV manufacturing network, with a particular focus on operational excellence, launch readiness, quality assurance, workforce leadership, and industrialization support for next-generation electric vehicle programs, including Neue Klasse-related production activities.

The role operates at the intersection of plant execution, strategic transformation, and cross-functional governance. The successful candidate will ensure that all relevant production units deliver against BMW Group expectations for safety, quality, cost, delivery, flexibility, and people leadership within the framework of the BMW Production System (BPS). This includes close coordination with Quality, Supply Chain, Industrial Engineering, HR Manufacturing, Finance, and plant leadership teams across the EMEA footprint.

A central mandate of the role is to secure robust Start of Production (SOP) performance, maintain disciplined quality gates, and drive production readiness in an increasingly complex EV environment shaped by technology transition, regulatory pressure, and workforce transformation. The role requires strong leadership presence in a co-determined environment, including effective collaboration with works councils and disciplined handling of Mitbestimmung requirements. The successful candidate must combine premium automotive production credibility with the ability to lead change at scale, strengthen digital manufacturing capability, and protect execution stability across a high-visibility manufacturing network.
key_responsibilities: Lead EMEA EV production performance across relevant plants and manufacturing value streams in alignment with BMW Group Production targets.
Ensure rigorous application and further development of the BMW Production System (BPS) across EV operations.
Own production readiness and execution stability for key EV launches and SOP milestones, including Neue Klasse-related ramp-up activities.
Drive achievement of safety, quality, cost, delivery, and productivity targets across the production network.
Maintain robust quality gate discipline in close partnership with Quality, Manufacturing Engineering, and plant leadership.
Provide executive oversight for capacity planning, throughput optimization, and recovery planning in the event of operational disruption.
Partner with Supply Chain to secure production continuity, escalation management, and supplier-related risk response.
Lead and develop senior plant and production leaders across the EMEA organization, ensuring succession depth and leadership consistency.
Drive digital manufacturing and Industry 4.0 adoption where it improves transparency, process control, and execution efficiency.
Manage the approved production budget envelope and support investment decisions through disciplined business-case evaluation.
Represent Production in cross-functional governance forums with HR, Finance, Quality, Purchasing, and Strategy stakeholders.
Ensure constructive and compliant collaboration with works councils and employee representatives in the context of organizational and operational change.
required_qualifications: University degree in engineering, industrial engineering, manufacturing, operations management, or a related technical discipline.
Extensive senior leadership experience in automotive or advanced manufacturing production environments.
Proven track record in EV or complex vehicle production management within a multi-plant or regional scope.
Demonstrated experience leading SOP, ramp-up, or industrialization programs in high-complexity manufacturing settings.
Deep understanding of production systems, lean manufacturing, and structured operational excellence frameworks such as BPS-equivalent environments.
Strong quality management experience, including familiarity with quality gates, process capability discipline, and automotive quality systems.
Experience leading large-scale production teams and senior leaders in matrixed, unionized, or co-determined environments.
Strong cross-functional stakeholder management capability across Quality, Supply Chain, HR, Finance, and Engineering.
Demonstrated financial acumen, including budget responsibility and the ability to translate operational decisions into business impact.
Fluency in English and strong professional German capability sufficient for executive and works council environments.
preferred_qualifications: Direct experience with Neue Klasse-related technologies, EV platform industrialization, or next-generation battery/e-drive production environments.
Experience in digital manufacturing, Industry 4.0, MES, analytics-enabled production control, or smart factory transformation.
Track record of leading production organizations through significant change while maintaining operational stability.
Experience managing complex supplier-related disruption, launch recovery, or production crisis response in the automotive sector.
International leadership experience across the EMEA manufacturing footprint.
Doctorate, MBA, or additional advanced qualification in engineering, manufacturing, or business leadership.
leadership_competencies: Demonstrates BMW-aligned leadership by combining operational discipline with forward-looking transformation capability.
Builds trust and execution alignment across functions, plants, and stakeholder groups in a complex manufacturing environment.
Leads through clarity, accountability, and fact-based decision-making while maintaining respect for co-determination and organizational context.
Drives performance improvement through structured problem-solving, production system excellence, and disciplined escalation.
Creates the conditions for sustainable people performance by developing leaders, strengthening talent pipelines, and fostering credible change leadership.
Translates strategic priorities into executable production outcomes with measurable business impact.

scope_and_scale.direct_reports: 6-9 senior production and plant leadership reports
scope_and_scale.total_workforce: Approx. 8,000-15,000 employees across direct and indirect production scope
scope_and_scale.annual_budget: Approx. EUR 1.2-2.0 billion operating budget influence across the production network
scope_and_scale.plants_overseen: Multiple EV-relevant production sites and associated regional manufacturing interfaces across EMEA
scope_and_scale.key_programs: Neue Klasse industrialization, EV SOP readiness, quality stabilization, BPS maturity, digital manufacturing enablement, workforce capability development

context_notes: The role is being filled at a strategically sensitive moment for BMW Group Production. The EV Division in EMEA is balancing launch intensity, productivity expectations, quality discipline, and organizational change against a backdrop of premium market pressure and rising regulatory complexity. Immediate priorities for the incoming leader will include safeguarding SOP readiness, strengthening execution consistency across the network, ensuring robust collaboration with Quality and Supply Chain, and improving the organization's ability to absorb digital and structural change without destabilizing core production performance. The role will also require careful navigation of works council expectations and Mitbestimmung processes as the production landscape evolves.
